meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 5–6.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Rikizo runs into Yamakawa near the bus stop and asks about his day.

lines.jp: やまかわさん、今日 どこへ 行きましたか。
バスで 駅へ 行きました。
毎日 バスで 来ますか。
はい、毎日 うちから バスで 来ます。

items.question: How does Yamakawa come every day?
items.explanation: B says 毎日うちからバスで来ます — 'Every day I come from home by bus.' うちから (from home) marks the starting point; バスで (by bus) marks the means of transport.

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Yamamoto-sensei asks Rikizo about his plans for next week.

lines.jp: りきぞさん、来週 どこへ 行きますか。
山へ 行きます。バスでは 行きません。車で 行きます。
山に 川が ありますか。
はい、山に 川が あります。道も いいですよ。いっしょに 来てください。

items.question: How will Rikizo travel to the mountain next week?
items.explanation: B says バスでは行きません。車で行きます — 'I won't go by bus. I'll go by car.' The negative ません contrasts bus (rejected) with car (chosen). 車 (car, N5.6) + で (by means of) states the transport method.

items.title: Conversation 3
items.context: Yamakawa tells Rikizo that an email about the mountain trip arrived.

lines.jp: りきぞさん、メールが 来ましたか。
はい、来ました。山の メールですね。
そうです。来週 車で 行きますか。
はい、友だちと 行きます。

items.question: Who will Rikizo go with next week?
items.explanation: B says 友だちと行きます — 'I will go with a friend.' と marks a companion for joint actions: 友だちと = 'together with a friend.'

sections.title: Part 2: Sentence Scramble
sections.instructions: Tap the words in the correct order to build the sentence.

items.q: Rikizo goes to the station by bus every day.
items.explanation: りきぞさん + は (topic) + 毎日 (every day) + バス + で (by bus, means) + 駅 + へ (to the station, direction) + 行きます. で marks the means of transport. へ marks direction. 毎日 can also open the sentence: 毎日りきぞさんはバスで駅へ行きます.

items.q: He went from the mountain to the river by car.
items.explanation: 山 + から (from, starting point) + 川 + まで (to, endpoint) + 車 + で (by car) + 行きました. The pattern から...まで describes the full range of a journey: 'from the mountain all the way to the river.'

items.q: Next week I will go to the supermarket with a friend.
items.explanation: 来週 (next week) + 友だち + と (with, companion) + スーパー + へ (to the supermarket) + 行きます. と marks a companion for joint actions. 来週 can also follow 友だちと: 友だちと来週スーパーへ行きます.

items.q: The mountain road is very nice.
items.explanation: 山 + の (attribute connector) + 道 + は (topic) + とても + いいです. の links 山 and 道: 'the mountain's road / the mountain road.' は marks 道 as the topic being described.

items.q: An email arrived from Yamakawa.
items.explanation: やまかわさん + から (from, source) + メール + が (subject of arrival event) + 来ました. から marks where the email came from. が marks メール as the thing that arrived — 来る is intransitive so its subject takes が.

items.q: I go from home to the station by bus.
items.explanation: うち + から (from home, starting point) + 駅 + まで (to the station, endpoint) + バス + で (by bus, means) + 行きます. The から...まで pattern marks the full span of the journey. で marks the means of transport.

items.q: Please come to the mountain by car.
items.explanation: 車 + で (by car, means) + 山 + へ (to the mountain, direction) + 来て (te-form of 来る) + ください (please). The て-form + ください pattern makes a polite request: 来てください = 'please come.' 行って (te-form of 行く, go) is a distractor — the sentence asks someone to 'come,' not 'go.'

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — Yamakawa's Mountain Drive
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the questions.

passage.jp: やまかわは 毎週 車で 山へ 行きます。
passage.en: Yamakawa goes to the mountain by car every week.

passage.jp: 山に 川が あります。
passage.en: There is a river at the mountain.

passage.jp: 山の 道は とても いいです。
passage.en: The mountain road is very nice.

passage.jp: 先週は 友だちと 山へ 行って、火山にも 行きました。
passage.en: Last week he went to the mountain with a friend and also went to the volcano.

passage.jp: 来週も 山へ 行きます。
passage.en: He will go to the mountain next week too.

questions.q: やまかわは なにで 山へ 行きますか。
choices: タクシーで 行きます
コンビニへ 行きます
questions.explanation: The passage says やまかわは毎週車で山へ行きます — 'Yamakawa goes to the mountain by car every week.' 車 (car, N5.6) + で (by means of) shows the transport method. The other options list different vehicles or an unrelated destination.

questions.q: 山に なにが ありますか。
choices: メールが あります
questions.explanation: The passage says 山に川があります — 'There is a river at the mountain.' に marks the location of existence with ある. が marks 川 as what exists there.

questions.q: 先週は だれと 行きましたか。
questions.a: 友だちと 行きました
choices: 友だちと 行きました
先生と 行きました
うちから 行きました
バスで 行きました
questions.explanation: The passage says 先週は友だちと山へ行って、火山にも行きました — 'Last week he went to the mountain with a friend and also went to the volcano.' と marks a companion: 友だちと = 'together with a friend.' Notice 行って (te-form connector) links two actions: going to the mountain, then going to the volcano.

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: 毎日 バスで [駅] へ 行きます。
items.explanation: 駅 (station) is read as eki. It has only an on-reading. 駅へ行きます = 'going to the station.' Introduced in N5.5 with Going & Places vocabulary.

items.q: 先週 [店] に 行きましたか。
items.explanation: 店 (shop/store) is read as mise (kun-reading). On-reading: てん. 先週店に行きましたか = 'Did you go to the store last week?' Introduced in N5.5 with Going & Places vocabulary.

items.q: [来週] も 山へ 行きますか。
items.explanation: 来週 (next week) is read as raishuu. 来 (rai, come/next, N5.5) + 週 (week, N5.4). Compare: 今週 (konshuu, this week), 先週 (senshuu, last week), 毎週 (maishuu, every week).

items.q: 車で [山] へ 行きました。
items.explanation: 山 (mountain) is read as yama (kun-reading). On-reading: さん, as in 富士山 (Fuji-san). Introduced in N5.6 with landscape vocabulary.

items.q: [川] から うちまで 車で 行きます。
items.explanation: 川 (river) is read as kawa (kun-reading). On-reading: せん. In compound place names, がわ is also used. Introduced in N5.6 with landscape vocabulary.

items.q: 山の [道] は とても いいです。
items.explanation: 道 (road/street) is read as michi (kun-reading). On-reading: どう. 山の道 = 'the mountain road' pairs two N5.6 kanji. Introduced in N5.6 with Landscape & Vehicles.

items.q: [車] で 山へ 行きますか。
items.explanation: 車 (car/vehicle) is read as kuruma (kun-reading). On-reading: しゃ. 車で = 'by car' — で marks the means of transport. Introduced in N5.6 with Landscape & Vehicles.

items.q: [火山] へも 行きましたか。
items.explanation: 火山 (volcano) is read as kazan. 火 (ka, fire, N5.2) + 山 (san/yama, mountain, N5.6). Both on-readings combine in this compound. Introduced in N5.6 as a landscape word.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: _______ で 山へ 行きます。
items.explanation: 車 (car) is the only means of transport in the list. 車で = 'by car' — で marks the means. 川 is a river, 道 is a road, 山 is a mountain destination — none of these work as transport 'by' which you travel.

items.q: 先週は スーパーへ _______。
items.explanation: 行きました is the polite past of 行く (to go). 先週 (last week) shows this is a past event. 行きます is present/future tense — wrong for last week. 来ます means 'come' — wrong direction for going to a supermarket. あります expresses inanimate existence, not movement.
ある (あります) is used for inanimate things: 川 (river) is a natural feature, not a living being. いる (います) is for animate beings (people, animals). 行きます and 来ます are motion verbs, not existence verbs.

items.q: うちから 駅 _______ バスで 行きます。
items.explanation: まで marks the endpoint of travel: 駅まで = 'all the way to the station.' The full pattern うちから駅まで = 'from home to the station.' から marks starting point — already used after うち. に marks a destination or time. の marks possession.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle to complete the sentence.

items.q: 山 _____ 川が あります。
items.explanation: に marks the location of existence with ある: 山に = 'at the mountain.' は would make 山 the topic with implied contrast. を marks a direct object, but ある is intransitive. が marks the subject — 川が already fills that role.

items.q: バス _____ 来ます。
items.explanation: で marks the means of transport: バスで = 'by bus.' に marks a destination or specific time, not a vehicle. は would make バス the topic rather than the means. を marks a direct object — 来る is intransitive and cannot take を.

items.q: うち _____ 駅まで バスで 行きます。
items.explanation: から marks the starting point: うちから = 'from home.' まで marks the endpoint — 駅まで is already present. に marks a destination or time point. を marks a direct object — not a starting point for movement.

items.q: 来週 友だち _____ 山へ 行きます。
items.explanation: と marks a companion for joint actions: 友だちと = 'together with a friend.' に marks a destination or time — 山へ already marks the destination. を marks a direct object. の marks possession — 友だちの means 'friend's'.

items.q: うちに 友だち _____ います。
items.explanation: が marks the subject of いる (to be present, animate): 友だちがいます = 'A friend is here.' は would make 友だち the topic — often implying contrast (友だちはいますが...). を marks a direct object, but いる is intransitive. に marks location — うちに already uses に for location.
